_comments.purpose: Source-of-truth for the app motion system. The /motion-docs route reads this file at runtime, and future export scripts can translate it into Lottie .json files.
_comments.lottie_translation: Each demo keyframe maps to a transform animation on one layer at 60fps for duration_ms. Frame number = duration_ms * fps / 1000 * t. Easing names map to standard Lottie bezier control points (in/out arrays).
_comments.composition_note: All demos render in a 1080x1080 composition at 60fps with the referenceShape centered. translateX and translateY values are composition pixels, then scaled down for smaller previews by dividing composition.width by preview.width.
_comments.scale_opacity_note: Scale and opacity are scale-invariant. They apply the same way at any preview size.

demos.title: Tap feedback: button
demos.subtitle: 150ms | ease-out | scale 0.96
demos.description: Buttons and icon buttons scale down to 0.96 on press. This feedback is fast, responsive, and immediate. Use it for primary and secondary CTAs, icon controls, and FABs.

demos.title: Press feedback: standard card
demos.subtitle: 200ms | ease-out | scale 0.985
demos.description: Standard cards use a subtler press scale than buttons. Use this for event cards, list items, and unselected radio cards.

demos.title: Press feedback: selected card
demos.subtitle: 200ms | ease-out | scale 0.99
demos.description: Selected cards already have emphasis, so their press feedback should be even smaller. Use this for selected radio cards.

demos.title: Reveal: popup emphasis
demos.subtitle: 200ms | ease-out | y:32->0, scale 0.98->1, opacity 0->1
demos.description: Use this for FansPill entry, selected-card emphasis, and popup or sheet content. The element enters softly with a small upward translation.

demos.title: Container transition: page or modal
demos.subtitle: 300ms | spring curve | x:48->0, opacity 0->1
demos.description: Use this for page transitions and modal enter or exit. The spring curve (0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) is the only easing with a light overshoot. Use it only for containers, never for interactive controls.

demos.title: Hero moment: illustration entry
demos.subtitle: 800ms | ease-out | scale 0.7->1.05->1, opacity 0->1
demos.description: Reserve this longer motion for expressive Lottie illustrations. It carries emotional weight, similar to a map fly-to moment in a location-based app.

demos.title: Anti-pattern: ease-in-out on an interactive control
demos.subtitle: Feels slow at the start and heavy at the end
demos.description: A symmetrical ramp makes tap feedback feel soft and delayed. Use ease-out for visible UI feedback.

demos.title: Anti-pattern: scale 0.94 on cards
demos.subtitle: Too aggressive for a premium interface
demos.description: Any value below 0.96 for buttons or 0.97 for cards feels too heavy. Premium motion should feel restrained.

demos.title: Anti-pattern: transition-colors without an explicit duration
demos.subtitle: Falls back to the wrong default timing and curve
demos.description: Tailwind transition-colors without a duration uses the default 150ms ease-in-out. Always define both duration and easing.

demos.title: Anti-pattern: spring curve on an interactive control
demos.subtitle: Overshoot makes tap feedback feel unstable
demos.description: The spring curve is only for containers. On buttons, it makes press feedback feel shaky instead of responsive.

demos.title: List stagger: card entry
demos.subtitle: 5 cards | 50ms stagger | 100ms delay | each card uses 200ms ease-out
demos.description: Event-card lists enter as a cascade on mount. This is a sequence, not a burst: the stagger creates a clear reading order.

demos.title: Radio selection: state change
demos.subtitle: 200ms background morph + 200ms dot fill, synchronized
demos.description: Use this for radio-card selection. Two things change together: the card background and the inner dot fill. Both use 200ms ease-out and fire from the same handler.

demos.title: Conclusion hero: choreographed entry
demos.subtitle: 800ms hero illustration + 200ms title reveal with a 400ms delay
demos.description: On the conclusion screen, the hero animation runs for 800ms. The title reveal starts 400ms after the hero begins, not after it ends. Treat this as one dominant motion with a supporting layer.

demos.title: Card to detail: measured FLIP with two faces
demos.subtitle: Geometry shell + source face + destination face
demos.description: Use this pattern when the source card and destination screen do not share the same visual structure. The outer shell owns only measured geometry: x, y, width, height, and borderRadius from the card rect to the PhoneFrame viewport. Inside the shell, two absolute inset-0 faces handle the visual swap: the source card face and the destination screen face. This prevents text and icon distortion, avoids a white intermediate state, and keeps the return motion continuous. Do not mix this pattern with layoutId for the same morph. Implementation rules: measure after releasing the PhoneFrame chrome with flushSync; keep the real card hidden with visibility:hidden until onExitComplete; let the overlay draw its own source face during close; keep the shell responsible only for geometry; use one shadow owner per phase, not stacked shell, source, and card shadows.

demos.title: Anti-pattern: mixing layoutId, FLIP, and corrective crossfade
demos.subtitle: Three owners for one transition create fragile behavior
demos.description: Avoid using layoutId, manual FLIP measurement, and corrective crossfade to solve the same morph. layoutId expects Framer Motion to own measurement. Manual FLIP expects the app to own measurement. Corrective crossfade hides visual differences after the fact. Mixing all three creates stale projections, wrong measurements when chrome changes, white flashes, duplicate shadows, and fragile handoffs. Choose one primary model for each transition.
